raw_consumer_statement:
While I acknowledge receipt of the {$1800.00} in temporary credits, this resolution is incomplete and does not address the full scope of fraud totaling significantly more in unauthorized charges that I have documented across multiple written disputes dated XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/year>. 



As previously stated : - I did not authorize the full set of transactions listed across my disputes. 
- Citi has failed to provide any documentation of its investigation or forensic analysis to determine whether card cloning or skimming occurred.

- Over half of the fraudulent charges I reported have not been credited, acknowledged, or explained in any correspondence.

In your response, you request a detailed list of additional charges. Please note that such a list has been repeatedly submitted via official letters, transaction reports, and attachments filed with the CFPB and included in my mailed correspondence. For convenience, I am again reattaching all relevant disputed transactions in this package. This includes files labeled : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Please treat this communication as a continuation of the original dispute under the same case reference. Under 15 U.S. Code 1666 and 12 CFR 1026.13, you are obligated to : 1. Complete your investigation within 90 days.

2. Provide a written determination with supporting evidence.

3. Refrain from any adverse reporting during this period.

In addition, Citi 's failure to respond comprehensively is in direct violation of the Fair Credit Billing Act and Electronic Fund Transfer Act. I also consider the lack of response or proof to be a continued obstruction of my consumer rights and may pursue damages accordingly through legal counsel. 



If I do not receive confirmation of full receipt of all prior and current documentation, as well as an update to the crediting of all remaining unauthorized transactions, I will escalate to : - A formal CFPB reopening request under 1031 of the Dodd-Frank Act, - Filing complaints with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency ( OCC ), and - Initiating civil action through legal counsel specializing in financial services violations. 



Please respond in writing confirming receipt of this rebuttal, along with an itemized status update of each disputed charge.
